title: HDX describe output
description: The full, facts-only self-description the `describe` verb emits (spec §10, R4). Mirrors the describe-local DTO 1:1: exactly {manifest, basins, fields, grids, time_extents, delineations}, additionalProperties:false at every level. It reports FACTS ONLY — there is no `conformant` key, no §14 check-outcome list, anywhere in this shape (that verdict is `validate`, a later milestone, spec §10). The manifest sub-object is EXACTLY the six floor fields (spec §11): no transform/role/semantic/provenance/derivable field is permitted (additionalProperties:false catches a 7th). Every `fields` entry — including the companion-mask `{field}_was_filled` and the `{source}_{variable}` patterns — carries the SAME ordinary key set {name, quadrant, dtype, units, grid_label}; the schema gives those name patterns no special key (spec §2). Discovery gaps are reported as facts: a basin with no extent has `time_extent: null`, an absent outlines rollup yields an empty `delineations` array. This shape is versioned IMPLICITLY by the manifest `format_version` ONLY (the spec §0 hard cut) — there is no separate schema-version field; a shape change requires a `format_version` bump.

basins.description: The discovered basin ids, in stable sorted order (spec §5).

fields.description: The unified field catalog (scalar ⊕ gridded, concatenated, no merge; spec §2/§5).

grids.description: The per-grid representative geometries (spec §7), one entry per discovered grid artifact.

time_extents.description: The per-basin ragged time-extent entries (spec §6.1), one per basin, in basin order.

delineations.description: The distinct neutral delineation labels (spec §9); an empty array is a recorded gap, never a verdict.

manifest.title: HDX manifest (describe view)
manifest.description: EXACTLY the six floor fields (spec §11). additionalProperties:false rejects a 7th (derivable / role / provenance) field; required lists all six. Mirrors `schemas/manifest.schema.json` but does not re-constrain values (describe reports the parsed manifest, it does not re-validate it).

format_version.description: The hard version cut (spec §0/§14 M2): "0.1" (geometry-mandatory) or "0.2" (geometry-optional). The describe shape is versioned by this value only.

name.description: Dataset identity — a generic producer string, not a role label (spec §11).

created_at.description: Dataset creation time as the verbatim RFC 3339 string (spec §14 M4).

producer_version.description: The tool/version that wrote the dataset (spec §11).

field.title: HDX field
field.description: An ordinary field catalog entry — EXACTLY {name, quadrant, dtype, units, grid_label} (spec §2). additionalProperties:false forbids any name-pattern-derived key (no mask / companion / source / variable / belongs_to). `units` and `grid_label` are nullable (string | null): absent → JSON null. `grid_label` is present (non-null) iff the field is gridded.

name.description: The verbatim producer field name (opaque; no role/semantic meaning attached).

dtype.description: The canonical physical dtype string (spec §2, closed set).

units.description: The recorded units string, or null when absent (never invented).

grid_label.description: The grid label this field lives on (gridded fields only), or null for scalar fields.

quadrant.title: HDX field quadrant
quadrant.description: The explicit 2×2 classification (spec §2): two independent axes emitted verbatim so a consumer never re-derives them from a packed string.

grid.title: HDX grid geometry
grid.description: A per-grid representative geometry (spec §7): label, cell-edge extent, signed per-axis resolution, pixel dimensions, recorded CRS.

grid_label.description: The grid label (the same neutral string the fields reference).

crs.description: The CRS string recorded by the grid artifact.

west.description: The west cell-edge coordinate (the NW origin's x).

north.description: The north cell-edge coordinate (the NW origin's y).

east.description: The east cell-edge coordinate (the far x).

south.description: The south cell-edge coordinate (the far y).

grid_resolution.title: HDX grid resolution
grid_resolution.description: The signed per-axis cell steps (spec §7); the y step is typically negative for a north-up grid.

time_extent_entry.title: HDX per-basin time-extent entry
time_extent_entry.description: A basin id paired with its time extent (spec §6.1). `time_extent` is null when the basin has no recorded extent — a reported GAP, never a verdict. `gridded_time_axis` is an ADDITIVE field (spec §6.2/§6.3): present only for a basin bearing gridded·dynamic geometry, OMITTED entirely for a pure-scalar basin (a scalar-only describe is byte-identical to its pre-0.2 shape).

basin_id.description: The basin folder id this entry belongs to.

time_extent.description: The basin's [start, end] span + its provenance, or null for a recorded gap.

gridded_time_axis.title: HDX per-basin gridded time axis
gridded_time_axis.description: The basin's gridded·dynamic `time` coordinate as the comparable i64-microseconds-since-epoch array plus its source CF provenance (spec §6.2/§6.3). ADDITIVE: present only for a gridded·dynamic-bearing basin; a pure-scalar basin omits this key. An inert fact, never a verdict.

micros.description: The full gridded `time` axis as i64 microseconds since the unix epoch (the int64 day-counts decoded then normalized; the representation `validate`'s T2/M6(b) compare on).

units.description: The source CF `units` string the axis is encoded under on disk (spec §6.3).

calendar.description: The source CF `calendar` string the axis is encoded under on disk (spec §6.3).

time_extent.title: HDX time extent
time_extent.description: A [start, end] span with the read-tier provenance that produced it (spec §6.1/§8).

start.description: The extent start as an RFC 3339 string.

end.description: The extent end as an RFC 3339 string.

source.description: Which read tier produced the extent (provenance fact, not a verdict).
